page_content:
URSP810 Contemporary Metropolitan Issues (3 Credits) Introduces Ph.D. students to current metropolitan issues. Focus is on the historical development of the issue, problem deﬁnition, methodological approaches to its study, methodological dilemmas, and the ways that different conclusions are translated into policy. Topics vary from semester to semester but include such topics as the spatial mismatch hpothesis, the impact of urban design and form on travel behavior, the impact of technology on urban form, the justiﬁcation for historic preservation, and sustainable development. Restriction: Must be in Urban and Regional Planning and Design (Doctoral) program.

URSP898 Pre-Candidacy Research (1-8 Credits) Selected topics in Urban Studies and Planning. Topics will vary with the instructor. Repeatable to: 6 credits if content differs.
